description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body and slightly bulging sides, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two wide, metallic hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a dark, weathered appearance, with visible grain and subtle texturing, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the body, with the top slightly recessed. On one side, there is a small, round wooden spigot or bung, indicating the barrel can be tapped for its contents. The metallic hoops are a dull gray, showing some wear and oxidation, and provide structural reinforcement. This type of barrel is commonly used for storage or transport of liquids such as water, wine, or oil in urban or rustic settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a small wooden spigot on one side, showing signs of age and outdoor use. The barrel is cylindrical with a bulging midsection and reinforced by two wide, gray metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view of the barrel showing the wooden staves and both metal hoops, with no spigot visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a small wooden spigot protruding from the right side, with the metal hoops and wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the spigot head-on at the center, with both metal hoops and the wood texture prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the spigot visible on the left, showing the barrel's curvature and reinforcement bands.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
